cot_content:
Okay, so the question is about how to tell your right and left earbuds apart without looking. The options are to tie a small knot either at the speaker end or the connecting end of the left-hand one.

First, I need to think about how earbuds are typically used. Each earbud has a speaker end that goes into the ear and a connecting end that attaches to the wire or cable. Now, if you want to mark the left earbud, where would a knot be useful?

If you tie a knot at the speaker end (option A), when you put the earbuds in your ears, the knot would be inside your ear. That might be uncomfortable or not very noticeable once in place. Also, you wouldn't be able to see or easily feel the knot once it's in your ear. So this might not be a practical way to distinguish them.

On the other hand, if you tie a knot at the connecting end (option B), that part is usually on the outside of your ear or along the cable near your neck. This would be more visible or tactile, making it easier to identify which earbud is the left one without having to look directly at them. When you're using the earbuds, you could feel for the knot on the outside, helping you know which side is which.

Another consideration: when you're not using the earbuds, the knot at the connecting end might help you visually identify which one is the left one when they're stored. A knot on the speaker end would be hidden when stored in a case or pocket.

So, based on practicality and ease of identification, tying a knot at the connecting end (option B) seems more effective for distinguishing the left earbud from the right one without looking.
label: minority
